title: Winter Recreation Small Business Recovery Act of 2025
short_title: Winter Recreation Small Business Recovery Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill helps small businesses that need snow to make money, like ski resorts and snowmobile rental shops. When there is not enough snow during winter, these businesses can now get government disaster help just like businesses hurt by floods or hurricanes. The government will make new rules about how to give this help and study ways to better support snow businesses when winters do not have enough snow. This means small businesses that depend on snow will have more protection when bad weather hurts their ability to make money.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Winter Recreation Small Business Recovery Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings
sections.text: Congress has learned the following facts. The American Meteorology Society defines snow drought as a time when there is much less snow on the ground than normal for that time of year. Snow drought can happen in two ways. It can be dry snow drought when there is less cold weather rain and snow than usual. It can also be warm snow drought when the weather gets normal amounts of moisture but it is too warm for snow to stick around. This happens when it rains instead of snows or when snow melts too early because of warm temperatures.

sections.title: Section 3 Definition of Disaster
sections.text: This section changes the Small Business Act to include snow drought as a type of disaster. The law already lists other disasters that small businesses can get help for. This change adds snow drought to that list. The head of the Small Business Administration must work with the Director of the National Weather Service to create rules for this new disaster type. These rules must be finished within ninety days after this law is passed. The Comptroller General of the United States must write a report for Congress. This report will look at what help the government currently gives to small businesses hurt by lack of snow. It will also suggest ways small businesses can change their business plans to survive when there is not enough snow. The report will recommend new laws or government actions to help businesses affected by low snow. It will also study whether the Small Business Administration can handle more disaster recovery work through the Economic Injury Disaster Loan program when businesses are hurt by lack of snow.
